relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Do not carry false report or join violent witness.
Do not let the many bend you toward harm or twisted justice.
Even compassion must not become partiality in judgment.
Return even your enemy’s wandering animal.
Help even the burdened donkey of one who hates you.
Do not bend justice against the poor.
Stay far from falsehood and innocent blood.
Bribes blind the seeing and twist the just word.
Do not oppress the sojourner; you know the sojourner’s soul.
Six years the land gives cultivated produce.
The seventh year releases land, poor, and beasts into provision.
The seventh day refreshes animals, servant’s child, and sojourner.
Guard your mouth from rival divine allegiances.
Three times yearly, festival gathers the people.
Unleavened Bread keeps the Egypt-exit alive.
Harvest and Ingathering honor field and season.
The males appear before Lord YHWH.
Festival offering is kept from leaven and delay.
Firstfruits come to YHWH’s house; life is not cooked in its mother’s milk.
A messenger guards the way to the prepared place.
Listen carefully, for the Name is in him.
Listening aligns the people with YHWH’s protection.
The messenger leads into the contested land.
Do not bow to the land’s gods or imitate their works.
Serve YHWH, and bread, water, and healing are blessed.
Life-fruitfulness and full days are promised.
YHWH’s dread goes before the people.
The hornet image drives out entrenched powers.
The land cannot be emptied too quickly without becoming wild against you.
Little by little the inheritance unfolds.
Borders are named as promised space.
Do not covenant with rival gods or their systems.
Idolatrous dwelling becomes a snare.

contemplative_note: Exodus 23 turns covenant outward: truthful speech, justice under pressure, enemy-animal mercy, sojourner empathy, Sabbath for land and vulnerable ones, and dangerous land promises that must be held with ethical sobriety.
